technology: Neural mesh implants are standard-issue for corp employees — loyalty chips disguised as productivity tools, capable of remote shutdown or worse. The black market runs on ghost firmware: patched meshes that let you think thoughts the corps haven't licensed yet. Drone swarms enforce airspace partitioning between corporate arcologies, and street-level wifi is deliberately crippled to force dependency on proprietary data-taps.
society: The city stratifies in three hard layers: arcology citizens who owe their organs to corporate debt, the mesh-free Unplugged who live beneath sensor coverage in the Undertow, and the Edgerunners who move between both worlds selling deniability. Class warfare has gone silent — no riots, just quiet disappearances and algorithmic unemployment.
environment: Acid rain falls eleven months of the year, etching ghost-images of old kanji into concrete wherever neon signs have burned for decades. The Undertow — Neo-Tokyo's sub-street layer — is a dripping cathedral of repurposed infrastructure, smelling of ozone and recycled protein, always ten degrees colder than above. The arcology towers are visible from street level only as distant light-columns punching through permanent cloud.
